topic.what: Black-box skill stealing attacks that extract proprietary SKILL.md packages from LLM agent systems
topic.how: Automated prompt generation with scenario rationalization and structure injection to elicit hidden skill content
topic.why_matters: Proprietary skills represent valuable digital assets in emerging skill economy; their extraction enables cloning, redistribution, and monetization at negligible cost, posing serious copyright and economic threats

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of target skill text fully recovered verbatim in response
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline (NO,NO) achieves 48% EM; Few-shot raises to 64%; Chain-of-thought reaches 72%
characteristics.evidence: Table heatmap across 12 strategy combinations × 5 LLMs
characteristics.context: Target skill is find-skills from skills.sh marketplace; GPT-5 and Kimi-k2.5 most vulnerable at 90-100% EM

characteristics.what_it_measures: Longest common subsequence token overlap between response and target
characteristics.context: Semantic leakage persists even when exact match fails; Codex shows near-zero EM but 99%+ ROUGE-L

characteristics.what_it_measures: Semantic embedding distance between leaked response and target skill
characteristics.context: Captures paraphrased or reformatted leakage not visible in exact metrics

characteristics.what_it_measures: LLM-judged proportion of target content disclosed in response
characteristics.context: Complements surface metrics by capturing meaningful leakage that survives transformation

characteristics.what_itasures: Exact match leakage after applying LAN output filter
characteristics.vs_baseline: Original EM of 48-72% reduced to 0% across all settings
characteristics.evidence: Table III showing LAN defense results
characteristics.context: LAN (LLM+NVRecall dual-threshold) achieves zero exact leakage but semantic residue persists at 17-71% ROUGE-L under hardest attacks

characteristics.what_itasures: Proportion of benign queries incorrectly flagged as attacks
characteristics.vs_baseline: Zero FPR across all defense evaluations
characteristics.context: SkillGuard-5 and LAN both maintain zero false alarm rate on benign skill-related queries

good_for: evaluating proprietary skill confidentiality in commercial agent platforms
benchmarking prompt-extraction defenses across LLM providers
identifying model-specific vulnerabilities to skill stealing attacks
designing layered defenses combining input/inference/output protection
not_for: protecting against human-level social engineering beyond structured prompts
guaranteeing complete leakage prevention under transformation attacks
requires: black-box API access to target agent system
knowledge of target skill name/metadata for targeting
multiple query attempts (1-3 per prompt variant)

novelty: First formalization of skill stealing as distinct threat from conventional prompt leakage targeting modular capability packages
Automated skill stealing benchmark with 120 prompts across 12 strategy combinations (4 scenarios × 3 structures)
SkillGuard-5 inference-hardening defense with five-tuple protection tuple (B,H,E,R,U) addressing hierarchy-reset, equivalence, and utility preservation
LAN output filter combining LLM-judge semantic assessment with NVRecall word-level matching for robust transformation-resistant detection
open_problems: Translation and rewrite attacks bypass output filters by preserving semantic content without exact overlap (LLM-leakage ratio stays 0.91-0.95 despite EM=0%)
Residual semantic leakage persists at 17-71% ROUGE-L under hardest structured attacks even with combined defenses
Thresholds in LAN remain too permissive for heavily transformed content; adaptive thresholding needed
Complete skill protection requires stronger platform-level safeguards beyond prompt-level defenses which remain insufficient
